Part B.key_evidence: Negative subtraction (isdiffpos=f) at a point source coincident with a PS1 star (sgscore1=0.97, distpsnr1=0.18", srmag1=20.15). classtar=0.85 and sharpnr=0.04 indicate a point-like source. ndethist=11 over deltajd~28 days at a highly covered field (ncovhist=877) indicates repeated variability at a stellar position.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: Stellar variable: the candidate is spatially coincident with a high-confidence PS1 point source of similar brightness (~20.1 mag r), showing multiple detections with a negative subtraction (fainter than reference) consistent with a variable star dimming.
Part B.alternative_analysis: An AGN is conceivable since PS1 sgscore can sometimes misclassify faint unresolved AGN, but sgscore1=0.97 and the compact PSF morphology strongly favor a stellar source over an AGN.
